mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate, increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, consumer spending contraction accelerates, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a synchronized collapse in aggregate demand, triggering broad equity sell-offs over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month drops in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply chain disruptions and factory idle capacity, causing short-term equity price declines within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO month-over-month changes during periods of VIX above 30.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.0 percent, further rate cuts provide diminishing marginal stimulus, causing equity markets to price in liquidity traps and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean return greater than 0.5% in all months where FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that outpaces productivity growth, compressing corporate margins and reducing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a negative coefficient with p-value greater than 0.10 in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real GDP growth directly boost corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as equity valuations adjust to higher earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward equity returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually, central banks prioritize price stability over growth, causing immediate equity price declines as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 215 threshold.
